data.title: Is It Time To Revisit Harrow (HROW) After Its Strong Multi Year Share Price Run?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Harrow's share price still makes sense after recent moves, you are not alone and the numbers give us plenty to talk about.
  • The stock trades at US$48.49, with a 7 day return of a 5% decline, a 30 day return of 8.3% and a 1 year return of 44.9%, while the 3 year return is very large and the 5 year return is also very large.
  • Recent news flow around Harrow has mainly focused on its position in the pharmaceuticals and biotech space and investor interest in how its current product portfolio and pipeline could influence future performance. These themes help frame the recent share price swings as the market weighs potential growth against the usual sector risks.
  • Our Simply Wall St valuation model currently gives Harrow a valuation score of 5 out of 6. This suggests it screens as undervalued on most of the checks we run. Next we will look at how different valuation approaches treat the stock before closing with an even more practical way to think about its value. Stay updated when valuation signals shift by adding Harrow to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our screener to discover other companies that fit your criteria.

Harrow delivered 44.9% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Pharmaceuticals industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Harrow Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model takes estimates of a company’s future cash flows and discounts them back to today’s dollars to arrive at an intrinsic value per share.

For Harrow, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow is a loss of $75.15 million, so the valuation leans heavily on future projections rather than current cash generation.

Analysts and internal estimates project free cash flow rising to $416.40 million in 2030, with intermediate projections between 2026 and 2035 ranging from $81.70 million to $660.05 million. Simply Wall St uses analyst estimates where available and then extrapolates the later years to complete the 10 year cash flow path.

Discounting these projected cash flows back to today results in an estimated intrinsic value of about $324.20 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $48.49, the model implies Harrow trades at roughly an 85.0% discount. This points to the stock screening as significantly undervalued on this measure.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Harrow is undervalued by 85.0%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 868 more undervalued stocks based on cash flows.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Harrow.

Approach 2: Harrow Price vs Sales

For companies that are not yet consistently profitable, the P/S ratio is often a useful yardstick because it compares what investors are paying to the revenue the business is already generating, without relying on earnings that can be negative or volatile.

Growth expectations and risk still matter because a higher growth outlook or lower perceived risk can justify a higher “normal” P/S multiple, while slower expected growth or higher risk usually supports a lower one. Context is therefore important when interpreting any single number.

Harrow currently trades on a P/S of 7.18x, compared with the Pharmaceuticals industry average of 4.81x and a peer group average of 8.22x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Harrow is 8.72x, which is its proprietary view of what the P/S should be given factors such as earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and company specific risks.

This Fair Ratio is often more informative than a simple peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for Harrow’s own growth, risk and profitability characteristics rather than assuming all companies deserve the same multiple. On this basis, Harrow’s actual P/S of 7.18x sits below the Fair Ratio of 8.72x, which suggests the shares screen as undervalued on this measure.

Result: UNDERVALUED
